name: Azelaic 15% Booster
hero: 15% azelaic at ₹ 549, in a thinner, faster-absorbing vehicle than The Ordinary's silicone suspension. The Indian value pick when you want clinical-grade rosacea / pigment support without the prescription pad.

scoreBreakdown.n: 15% — clinical efficacy plateau for rosacea / pigment.
Lighter than The Ordinary; still slightly tacky.
Class evidence solid; product itself not RCT'd.
₹ 549 / 30 mL — among the best cost-per-active in this catalogue.

ingredients.note: Clinical efficacy dose. Approaches Rx Finacea.

useCases.b: OTC alternative to Finacea Gel. Once-daily PM.
Pairs cleanly with niacinamide and SPF; safer than tret in pregnancy.
Layer with TXA / niacinamide. PM only.

alts.name: Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
alts.note: Lower active; thicker silicone vehicle; same price tier.

alts.name: Azelaic Topical Acid 10%
alts.note: Cleaner vehicle; harder to source in India.

faq.a: Same active concentration; Finacea's gel vehicle delivers slightly better and is MHRA / FDA validated. For mild-moderate cases, OTC 15% is a sensible first-line that's been used clinically for years.
Often, weeks 1-2. Apply over thin moisturiser; build up to nightly. Settles by week 3 in most users.
One of the safest pigment / acne actives in pregnancy. Use freely; it's a category B ingredient class.
Alternate nights initially. Once tolerated, PM stack of azelaic 30 minutes after retinoid is acceptable, with moisturiser between.

sources.n: Sieber MA, Hegel JK. Azelaic acid: properties and mode of action. Skin Pharmacol Physiol 2014.
Liu RH et al. Azelaic acid in pregnancy. Am J Clin Dermatol 2020.
Verallo-Rowell VM et al. Azelaic 20% vs hydroquinone 4% in melasma. Acta Derm Venereol 1989.
